title: QA: read_lsp_response hard-codes 100-message scan limit and conflates errors
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/tools/lsp.rs:276-322
## Violation
`read_lsp_response` loops "up to 100 messages" looking for the matching id. After exhausting it returns a single string error; the legitimate "server hangs" path and the "server is chatty with diagnostics" path are conflated. Servers like clangd routinely emit dozens of diagnostics before completing — 100 is an unprincipled magic number.

Quoted (lsp.rs:280-322):
```
    for _attempt in 0..100 {
        // Read headers
        let mut content_length: usize = 0;
        loop {
            let mut line = String::new();
            reader
                .read_line(&mut line)
                .map_err(|e| format!("Read error: {e}"))?;
...
    Err(format!(
        "LSP server did not respond to request {expected_id} after 100 messages"
    ))
```

Also: the inner header-parsing loop is tightly coupled to the outer id-matching loop. SRP violation; the function does 3 jobs (frame parsing, message routing, timeout enforcement).
## Mandated refactor
1. Split into `read_lsp_frame(reader) -> Result<Value, FrameError>` and `wait_for_response(reader, id, deadline) -> Result<Value, WaitError>`.
2. Replace the 100-message cap with a wall-clock deadline (`Instant`-based) configurable per-request.
3. Distinguish error types via `thiserror` — `BadFrame`, `ServerClosed`, `Timeout`, `ProtocolError` — not stringly-typed `Result<_, String>`.
4. Route notifications (`$/progress`, `textDocument/publishDiagnostics`) into an explicit notification handler instead of silently discarding them.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
